parts.title: English Teachers
prompts: Create a lesson plan for a [grade X] English class that meets the curriculum goals for [specific focus, e.g. oral communication], including three activities and a warm-up.
Give me five strategies for motivating a [grade X] student who is uncertain about speaking English, with an encouraging idea for each.
Write a short guide for teaching a [grade X] class the basics of [specific skill, e.g. writing a story], with three steps and an example.
Suggest three ways to integrate [specific theme, e.g. culture in English-speaking countries] into an English lesson for a [grade X] class, with one activity for each.
Create a list of five short encouraging phrases in English to build the confidence of a [grade X] student during a language exercise.
Give me an example of a routine for starting an English lesson with a [grade X] class, with three steps and a simple conversation exercise.
Suggest three ways to use everyday topics (e.g. [specific subject, e.g. leisure]) in an English lesson for a [grade X] class, with one exercise for each.
Write a short template for a 10-minute closing routine for an English lesson with a [grade X] class, with three components (e.g. reflection, showcase).
Give me five ideas for making an English lesson for a [grade X] class more playful, with a focus on [specific goal, e.g. vocabulary], and one game for each.
Create a list of three short reflection questions in English for a [grade X] class after a lesson on [specific theme, e.g. friendship], to capture their thoughts.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with Social Studies teachers at a [primary school] to connect English to [specific theme, e.g. global issues], with one idea for each.
Give me an example of a simple assessment of a [grade X] class's oral skills in English, with three criteria.
Write a list of five resources (e.g. apps, books) for an English lesson with a [grade X] class that support [specific goal, e.g. reading comprehension].
Suggest three ways to adapt an English lesson for a [grade X] student with [specific need, e.g. dyslexia], with one solution for each.
Create a template for a weekly English plan for a [grade X] class, with three focus areas (e.g. listening, speaking, writing).
Give me five ideas for using minimal resources in an English lesson with a [grade X] class to practise [specific goal, e.g. dialogues], with one example for each.
Suggest three ways to introduce a [grade X] class to [specific genre, e.g. poetry], with one hands-on exercise for each.
Write a short guide for managing a quiet [grade X] class during an English lesson, with three steps and one conversation starter.
Give me an example of an English lesson for a [grade X] class that combines language with [specific theme, e.g. the environment], with three components.
Create a list of three short ways to collaborate with the school library at a [primary school] to reinforce [specific goal, e.g. reading], with one example for each.
Create a lesson for a [grade X] class in which they write a short story in English about [specific theme, e.g. a future world], with three steps and one learning objective.
Give me five ways to make a listening exercise more engaging for a [grade X] class, with one creative twist for each.
Write a short guide for teaching a [grade X] class to deliver a simple presentation in English, with three steps and one example topic.
Suggest three ways to connect an English lesson for a [grade X] class to [specific theme, e.g. digital communication], with one exercise for each.
Create a list of five short conversation starters in English for a [grade X] class that build [specific goal, e.g. spontaneity], with a description for each.
Give me an example of a routine for closing an English lesson for a [grade X] class with a short dialogue, with three steps.
Suggest three ways to use digital tools (e.g. [specific tool, e.g. Quizlet]) in an English lesson for a [grade X] class, with one task for each.
Write a short template for a 5-minute reflection in English after a lesson with a [grade X] class, with three questions about their learning.
Give me five ideas for integrating games into an English lesson for a [grade X] class, with a focus on [specific goal, e.g. grammar], and one game for each.
Create a list of three short ways to support a [grade X] student who is struggling with [specific skill, e.g. pronunciation] in English, with one example for each.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with music teachers at a [primary school] to connect English to [specific theme, e.g. song lyrics], with one idea for each.
Give me an example of a simple plan for teaching a [grade X] class to write a letter in English over one lesson, with three steps.
Write a list of five simple texts or songs for a [grade X] class that reinforce [specific goal, e.g. vocabulary], with a rationale for each.
Suggest three ways to manage a [grade X] class that is reluctant to participate in an English lesson, with one method for each.
Create a template for a term plan for English for a [grade X] class, with three focus areas (e.g. reading, writing, speaking) and timeframes.
Give me five ideas for setting up a simple classroom debate in English with a [grade X] class, with minimal preparation, and one topic for each.
Suggest three ways to introduce a [grade X] class to [specific language concept, e.g. idiomatic expressions], with one exercise for each.
Write a short guide for supporting a [grade X] student with [specific need, e.g. language difficulties] during an English lesson, with three steps.
Give me an example of a lesson for a [grade X] class that combines English with [specific theme, e.g. technology], with three components.
Create a list of three short ways to collaborate with the student council at a [primary school] to reinforce [specific goal, e.g. English communication], with one example for each.
